steps.title: Use all required facts
steps.content: The student's goal is to place the 1821 Declaration in the context of de Leon's changing political beliefs. The relevant chain is that de Leon had long opposed independence, changed his mind after Colonel Aranda's 1820 revolt, and then wrote the Declaration of Independence in 1821. A complete answer must include both the political shift and the declaration.

steps.title: Keep the context complete
steps.content: Choice A mentions the revolt and de Leon's previous opposition, but it never connects the change to the 1821 Declaration. Choice C gives the wrong cause, since the notes say his mind changed after the 1820 revolt, not when the king repealed the 1812 constitution. Choice D links the revolt to the Declaration only speculatively and omits de Leon's changing beliefs. The complete sentence links prior opposition, changed view, and authorship. The answer is B.
